business_domains.description: Core domain covering all arithmetic operations supported by the calculator.

business_rules.description: The calculator supports exactly four arithmetic operations: Add, Subtract, Multiply, and Divide.
actions: Present the user with a selectbox containing exactly the options: Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide

business_rules.description: When the application loads, the default selected operation is 'Add' (index 0).
conditions: User has not changed the operation selector

business_rules.description: When operation is 'Add', the result is computed as num1 + num2 and displayed with the '+' symbol.
When operation is 'Subtract', the result is computed as num1 - num2 and displayed with the '-' symbol.
When operation is 'Multiply', the result is computed as num1 * num2 and displayed with the '×' symbol.
When operation is 'Divide' and num2 is non-zero, the result is computed as num1 / num2 and displayed with the '÷' symbol.
exceptions: Division by zero is blocked before this action executes (see BR-007)

business_rules.description: When the Divide operation is selected and the second number equals zero, the application must display an error message and halt further execution. No result value is computed or displayed.
actions: Display error: 'Division by zero is not allowed.'
Call st.stop() to terminate script execution immediately

business_domains.description: Rules governing how user input is captured, defaulted, and formatted.

business_rules.description: Both number inputs default to 0.0 when the application first loads or is reset.
Both number inputs accept and display values with up to six decimal places.
Computation is only triggered when the user explicitly clicks the 'Calculate' button. Widget changes alone do not produce a result.

business_domains.description: Rules governing how computation results and metadata are presented to the user.

business_rules.description: The result is displayed in the format '<num1> <symbol> <num2> = <result>' inside a green success banner.
Each operation maps to a specific Unicode symbol used in the result expression: Add → '+', Subtract → '-', Multiply → '×' (U+00D7), Divide → '÷' (U+00F7).
actions: Assign symbol string based on selected operation

business_rules.description: After a successful computation, the structured computation details (first_number, second_number, operation, result) are made available in a collapsible 'Computation details' expander panel.

workflows.description: End-to-end workflow from user input to result display.

steps.name: Load Application
steps.description: Page loads with default values (num1=0.0, num2=0.0, operation='Add').

steps.name: Enter Inputs
steps.description: User fills in First number, Second number, and selects an operation.

steps.name: Submit Form
steps.description: User clicks 'Calculate'. Streamlit re-runs the script with submitted=True.

steps.name: Evaluate Operation
steps.description: The script branches on the operation value and computes the result.

steps.name: Display Result
steps.description: If successful, render the result banner and expander. If division-by-zero, render error and stop.

workflows.happy_path: User enters two numbers, selects Add/Subtract/Multiply, clicks Calculate → result shown.

error_paths.scenario: User selects Divide with second number = 0
error_paths.outcome: Error message displayed, execution halted, no result shown
